Enhance the existing GigAid application to implement a high-conversion “First Booking” onboarding flow for users arriving from pre-generated, unclaimed booking pages.

IMPORTANT:
This must NOT change the existing booking page experience for already-signed-up customers or already-claimed booking pages.

The current customer booking page flow must remain unchanged.

This new flow applies ONLY to booking pages that are:

* pre-generated by the Growth Engine
* unclaimed
* intended for acquisition / claim conversion

Build this into the GigAid app (NOT the growth engine).

---

# 🎯 CORE GOAL

Drive unclaimed-page users to complete ONE action:

👉 Send their booking link to a real customer

Do NOT expose full product features before this action.

Do NOT alter the current booking experience for normal existing GigAid users.

---

# 🧩 PAGE MODE REQUIREMENT (CRITICAL)

Keep the existing route:

GET /book/:pageId

But add conditional behavior:

* If booking page is claimed:
  → render the EXISTING customer-facing booking page exactly as it works today
* If booking page is unclaimed:
  → render the NEW acquisition / claim flow described below

Do NOT replace the existing claimed booking page design.
Do NOT break current customer booking page UX.

---

# 🧩 PART 1 — UNCLAIMED BOOKING PAGE EXPERIENCE ONLY

Applies ONLY when:

* booking_page.claimed = false
  OR
* booking_page.source = "growth_engine"

## Route:

GET /book/:pageId

## UI REQUIREMENTS FOR UNCLAIMED PAGES ONLY

Render a simple, clean acquisition page with:

### Header (CRITICAL)

Dynamic title:

* Use category + location
  Examples:
* “Brooklyn Moving Services”
* “Hoboken Cleaning Service”

### Main Section

Headline:
“This page was created for your business”

Subtext:
“Customers can book you and pay a deposit instead of texting back and forth”

### CTA (PRIMARY)

Button:
“Claim this page”

### Trust Signal

Small line:
“Contractors like you are using this to get booked faster”

---

# 🧩 PART 2 — CLAIM FLOW

## Route:

POST /api/claim-page

## FLOW (MULTI-STEP, LOW FRICTION)

### Step 1

Prompt:
“Is this your business?”

Button:
“Yes, claim it”

### Step 2

Minimal form:

* Name (optional)
* Phone (pre-filled if available)
* Service (pre-filled from category)

Button:
“Continue”

### Step 3

On submit:

* Mark page as claimed
* Associate with user account (or create lightweight user record if needed)
* Preserve compatibility with existing GigAid account model

## IMPORTANT RULES

* DO NOT require password/email upfront
* DO NOT redirect to dashboard immediately
* Keep flow under 10 seconds

---

# 🧩 PART 3 — FIRST BOOKING SCREEN (UNCLAIMED FLOW ONLY)

After claim, redirect to:

GET /first-booking/:pageId

This route is ONLY for newly claimed acquisition pages.
It must NOT affect existing signed-up users unless they arrived through this unclaimed-page claim flow.

## UI REQUIREMENTS

Headline:
“Send this to your next customer”

Primary CTA:
“Copy your booking link”

Secondary CTA:
“Send via text”

Use device SMS intent if possible.

Microcopy:
“Instead of texting back and forth, just send this and they can book + pay a deposit”

## HARD CONSTRAINTS

* DO NOT show dashboard
* DO NOT show settings
* DO NOT show navigation menu
* Only show:

  * Copy link
  * Send link

---

# 🧩 PART 4 — ACTION TRACKING

Track events for unclaimed-page onboarding flow only:

* page_viewed
* page_claimed
* link_copied
* link_shared

Store timestamps.

Do not interfere with existing analytics for normal customer booking pages.

---

# 🧩 PART 5 — TRIGGER SYSTEM (NUDGES)

If newly claimed user does NOT copy/share link:

## After 10 minutes

Send SMS:
“Send your booking link to your next customer — it saves a ton of back and forth”

## After 24 hours

Send SMS:
“Most people get their first booking within a day after sharing their link”

Implementation options:

* Option A: call Growth Engine webhook
* Option B: use internal scheduler

These nudges should apply ONLY to the new acquisition / first-booking flow.

---

# 🧩 PART 6 — UX PRINCIPLES

Apply these rules ONLY to the unclaimed acquisition flow:

1. Use plain language
2. Focus on outcome:

   * “Get booked”
   * “Get paid upfront”
3. Keep UI minimal
4. Remove distractions
5. Max 2 actions per screen

Do not force these principles onto the existing claimed customer booking page if it would disrupt current behavior.

---

# 🧩 PART 7 — BACKEND STRUCTURE

Ensure booking_pages table supports:

* id
* phone
* category
* location
* claimed (boolean)
* claimedAt (timestamp)
* source (nullable string, e.g. "growth_engine" or "user_created")

If source does not exist yet, add it.

Use this to distinguish:

* acquisition pages
* existing user-created pages

---

# 🧩 PART 8 — OPTIONAL ENHANCEMENTS

For unclaimed acquisition pages only:

* Pre-fill service description based on category
* Add simple availability slots
* Add “Deposit required” label

Do not overwrite existing settings or page content for already-claimed pages.

---

# 🎯 FINAL GOAL

This system must:

* Convert cold traffic into claimed users
* Push newly claimed users to share their booking link immediately
* Increase conversion rate from ~5% to 15%+
* Preserve the current booking page experience for existing GigAid customers

---

# ⚠️ NON-NEGOTIABLE SAFETY CONSTRAINT

Do NOT redesign or replace the existing claimed booking page flow.
This project is an extension for unclaimed acquisition pages only.

Existing customers must continue seeing the current booking page UX unchanged.
